description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build and fstest. In the build stage define jobs: 'aur package for arch' using image archlinux:base that runs pacman to install build tools, creates a build user, executes 'makepkg -s' as that user and copies the resulting *.tar.zst, publishing them as artifacts; 'build on arch', 'build on fedora', and 'build on ubuntu' each use their respective images (archlinux:base, fedora, ubuntu:22.04) to install meson, cmake, gcc, fuse3 and other dependencies, then run the shared build script (mkdir -pv build; meson setup --buildtype=release --prefix=/usr build .; meson compile --jobs=$(nproc) -C build; ./build/apitest) and store the built binary at build/marcfs as an artifact; 'deb package for ubuntu' uses ubuntu:22.04, installs dh-make and related tools, runs dh_make and dpkg-buildpackage to produce *.deb files as artifacts; 'rpm package for fedora' uses fedora, installs rpm build tools, creates a source tarball, runs rpmbuild to generate *.rpm files as artifacts; 'static binary universal build' runs on alpine, installs static build dependencies, builds static libraries and the project, then builds the binary and saves it as an artifact. In the fstest stage define jobs: 'fstest on arch', 'fstest on fedora', 'fstest on ubuntu', and 'static binary test' each use the same image as their corresponding build, install test dependencies, create a test user, copy the built marcfs binary and fstest.py script, then execute 'python3 fstest.py -v' (or via su with environment whitelist) as that user, and after the test run remove the test user. All jobs reference MARCFS_TEST_USERNAME and MARCFS_TEST_PASSWORD variables supplied from CI secrets.
